scenario: getStore("desktop-plugin-config") throws because the store name is invalid or electron-store fails to create the file (disk full, permissions, cwd not set)
fallback_behavior: Unhandled exception propagates through the IPC handler — no try/catch wraps the handler body. Promise rejects with electron-store's error. Renderer-side catches it (dialog-manage-plugins.tsx loadConfigs catch block), sets empty configs. Plugin features silently disabled.
fix: Wrap getStore("desktop-plugin-config") in try/catch inside the IPC handler, return { configs: [], dropped: 0 } on failure so the error surface is clean and the IPC promise resolves gracefully instead of rejecting.

scenario: store.get("configs") returns undefined on cold cache / first run because the key doesn't exist yet in the electron-store file.
fallback_behavior: validateAndFilterPluginConfigs(undefined) — the guard if (!Array.isArray(raw)) returns { configs: [], dropped: 0 }. Clean fallback. Renderer shows the empty state UI ("No plugins installed. Click Install to add one.").

scenario: Renderer calls window.api.getDesktopPluginConfig() before registerIpcHandlers() executes. No handler registered for 'get-desktop-plugin-config' at the Electron level.
fallback_behavior: Electron rejects the ipcRenderer.invoke() with an error like 'No handler registered for "get-desktop-plugin-config"'. Renderer catches this via try/catch in dialog-manage-plugins.tsx (loadConfigs) and catch(() => undefined) in plugin-loader.ts. Plugin features silently disabled. If dialogs open before IPC is ready, they show empty state with no error feedback.
fix: Guard all IPC calls behind an initialization promise that resolves after registerIpcHandlers completes. Renderer should await a 'ready' signal before invoking plugin handlers.

scenario: window.api.getDesktopPluginConfig?.() evaluates to undefined because the preload script failed to load or didn't expose this specific method (browser dev mode, CSP block, contextIsolation issue).
fallback_behavior: Optional chaining `?.()` returns undefined. Nullish coalescing `?? { configs: [], dropped: 0 }` kicks in. Plugin loader silently returns empty config list. No user-facing error, no console warning.
note: The ?.() handles the undefined method case cleanly, and the catch(() => undefined) handles the IPC rejection case. The silent swallow of errors means a developer debugging in browser dev mode gets no diagnostic output.

scenario: window.api.getDesktopPluginConfig() called WITHOUT optional chaining. If window.api is undefined (browser dev mode, no Electron) or getDesktopPluginConfig is absent from the api object, this throws TypeError.
fallback_behavior: TypeError propagates to the enclosing try/catch block. loadConfigs sets empty configs and dropped = 0. The UI shows blank state. No user-facing error message, no console warning. The error is silently swallowed.
fix: Add optional chaining (window.api?.getDesktopPluginConfig?.()) and surface a console.warn when the API is unavailable so developers get diagnostic output during browser dev mode.

scenario: window.api.getDesktopPluginConfig() called WITHOUT optional chaining in the submit handler. If window.api.getDesktopPluginConfig is undefined, throws TypeError.
fallback_behavior: TypeError caught by the try/catch in submit(). setError("Failed to install plugin") shows a user-facing error message in the alert box. This is actually adequate because the user sees feedback, unlike the silent failure in loadConfigs.
note: The submit handler catches the error and displays "Failed to install plugin" to the user, which is proper error handling despite the missing optional chaining.

dependency: i18n resolution — ALL text in dialog-manage-plugins.tsx and dialog-install-plugin.tsx
scenario: All 22 i18n keys (e.g., "desktop.plugin.manage.title", "desktop.plugin.install.npmLabel") are passed as raw string literals directly to component props instead of being resolved through the t() function. Example: <Dialog title="desktop.plugin.manage.title" description="desktop.plugin.manage.description">
fallback_behavior: The raw string keys are rendered verbatim in the UI. The dialog title displays "desktop.plugin.manage.title" instead of "Plugin Manager". All text content, labels, placeholders, and accessible labels show i18n key identifiers instead of human-readable text. The i18n system's t() function is never called for these strings.
fix: Replace all raw i18n key strings with t() calls. Every occurrence of a "desktop.plugin.*" string must be wrapped: <Dialog title={t("desktop.plugin.manage.title")} description={t("desktop.plugin.manage.description")}>. Similarly for all button text, labels, placeholders, and aria attributes. The i18n context (useI18n/t) is already available in the renderer — it just isn't being used.

scenario: <Switch label={entry.enabled ? "desktop.plugin.list.enabled" : "desktop.plugin.list.disabled"}> passes a `label` prop to Switch, but SwitchProps (packages/ui/src/components/switch.tsx) does NOT declare a `label` prop. The interface extends Kobalte's Switch props via `ParentProps<ComponentProps<typeof Kobalte>>`, but Switch's visible label is rendered through children (<Kobalte.Label>{children}</Kobalte.Label>), not a prop.
fallback_behavior: The `label` prop passes through to the underlying Kobalte Switch via `{...others}` spread. Kobalte may forward it as an HTML attribute (rendering a useless attribute on the DOM element) or ignore it entirely. The visible label element (<Kobalte.Label>) renders nothing because Switch receives no children. The aria-label on the switch input may get the string value, but visible text content for "Enabled" / "Disabled" is lost. Combined with the i18n issue, the raw key string "desktop.plugin.list.enabled" would appear if children were properly used, but currently nothing renders.
fix: Pass label text as children: <Switch checked={entry.enabled} onChange={() => toggleEnabled(entry.name, entry.enabled)}>{t(entry.enabled ? "desktop.plugin.list.enabled" : "desktop.plugin.list.disabled")}</Switch>. Add hideLabel if the label should be screen-reader-only.

scenario: Raw i18n keys passed as label and placeholder props to TextField: <TextField label="desktop.plugin.install.npmLabel" placeholder="desktop.plugin.install.npmPlaceholder">. TextField.Label renders local.label as-is — no i18n resolution.
fallback_behavior: The label "desktop.plugin.install.npmLabel" and placeholder "desktop.plugin.install.npmPlaceholder" appear literally in the UI. These are not translated. Kobalte's TextField.Label and placeholder attributes render raw strings.
fix: Same root cause as #7: all i18n key strings must be wrapped with t(). label={t("desktop.plugin.install.npmLabel")} placeholder={t("desktop.plugin.install.npmPlaceholder")}.

dependency: Other locale files (fr.ts, zh.ts, de.ts, es.ts, da.ts, ja.ts, ko.ts, pl.ts, ru.ts, uk.ts, ar.ts, no.ts, br.ts, bs.ts, zht.ts)
scenario: None of the 15 non-English locale files in packages/desktop/src/renderer/i18n/ include the 22 new "desktop.plugin.*" keys. They stop at "desktop.cli.failed.message".
fallback_behavior: The i18n build() function creates `base` from merged appEn + desktopEn (which includes plugin keys), then merges locale-specific keys on top. Since locale files lack plugin keys, they fall through to the English values. Non-English users see English text for all plugin management UI. TypeScript Dictionary type includes the keys because it derives from typeof desktopEn (which has them). TypeScript compiles cleanly. Functional behavior is correct, but localization coverage is incomplete.
note: This is functionally adequate — the fallback-to-English pattern is standard i18n practice. Missing translations are not a bug. The fix is to add translated keys to each locale file before shipping.

scenario: DialogProps.title is typed as `JSXElement` (SolidJS type = string | number | Element | ...). The code passes raw string i18n keys. JSXElement accepts strings, so there's no TypeScript error.
fallback_behavior: No type mismatch. The string renders literally in the dialog title. This is incorrect behavior (shows key instead of translated value) but correct typing. The issue is semantic (missing t() call), not a type-system issue.
note: The title typing itself is fine. This is only a problem because the keys aren't resolved. If t() were used, the JSXElement type accepts the resolved string or a <span> wrapper equally.

dependency: Button component onClick in dialog-install-plugin.tsx line 143
scenario: Cancel button has no onClick handler: <Button variant="ghost" disabled={installing()}>{"desktop.plugin.install.cancel"}</Button> — missing the onClick={cancelInstall} that's present in dialog-manage-plugins.tsx.
fallback_behavior: The cancel button renders but does nothing when clicked. User cannot dismiss the install dialog without submitting or refreshing. This is a logic bug in the cancel button, not a dependency failure per se.
note: This is not strictly a dependency failure, but was discovered during the stress analysis. The cancel button in dialog-install-plugin.tsx is missing its onClick handler.
